stories.title: Russian strike on Kyiv region kills 4 and wounds 15, with peace talks stalled
stories.description: The strikes comes after the United States paused ceasefire talks between Russia and Ukraine due to the war with Iran.

stories.title: How the conflict in Iran is affecting global markets
stories.description: Escalation of the Iran conflict is sending shockwaves through global markets — driving up oil, fuel, and commodity prices, stoking inflation and recession risks worldwide. We hear from three NPR reporters in Europe, Asia and Russia.

stories.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
stories.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S. had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

stories.title: These are the casualties and cost of the war in Iran 2 weeks into the conflict
stories.description: The war in Iran has already cost the U.S. billions of dollars. Here's the impact by the numbers.

stories.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy concerned as U.S. suspends Russia sanctions
stories.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy meets French President Emmanuel Macron in Paris to increase pressure on Russia, as the U.S. suspends oil sanctions.

stories.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
stories.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: Iranians react to new supreme leader's first address
stories.description: People in Iran are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV.

stories.title: 'There's no hiding place on a ship': The sailors stranded near Iran
stories.description: Drones, cruise missiles and fighter jets have become a common sight for many sailors stranded on ships in the Gulf.

stories.title: Bodies of 84 Iranian sailors killed in US torpedo strike to be repatriated
stories.description: The seamen died when Iranian warship the Iris Dena was sunk on 4 March by a US submarine near Sri Lanka.

stories.title: 'Follow the smoke': BBC spends day with emergency teams as Israel strikes south Lebanon
stories.description: The BBC visited the city of Nabatieh, witnessing the pace and scale of Israel's bombing as its war with Hezbollah continues to escalate.

stories.title: Tracking the wave of ship attacks that has choked off Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Six ships have been reportedly attacked in the Gulf in less than 48 hours, bringing the total vessels attacked during the war to 18.

stories.title: Iran's new supreme leader vows to block Strait of Hormuz in first statement
stories.description: A message attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei also says Iran will continue targeting US bases in the region.

stories.title: Emirati minister tells BBC Iran must end strikes on Gulf
stories.description: The UAE says more than 1,800 drones and missiles have been projected at the country since the war began.

stories.title: 'What if we're left with ruins?': Doubts creep in for Iranians who supported war
stories.description: Teachers, engineers and shopkeepers tell the BBC they fear Iran falling into chaos.

stories.title: Israel pounds Beirut suburbs and south Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket barrage
stories.description: Some Israeli strikes also hit other parts of the Lebanese capital, with 12 people reportedly killed along the seafront.

stories.title: British man charged in Dubai for alleged filming of Iranian missiles
stories.description: Foreign Office officials are understood to be supporting his family.

stories.title: 'Fingers on the trigger': Deadly warnings for Iranians being urged to take action
stories.description: Iran's police chief has warned that his forces would treat anyone taking to the streets "at the enemy's request" as an enemy.

stories.title: Questions mount for Hegseth over possible US involvement in strike on Iranian school
stories.description: Democrats have written to ask the defence secretary whether the US was responsible, but he says the issue is being investigated.

stories.title: Iranian strikes, Hezbollah rockets make regular life in Israel ‘simply impossible’
stories.description: Attacks on northern Israel have been “constant” and “relentless”, says FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky, reporting from Jerusalem. Iranian missiles combined with Hezbollah rocket attacks are making regular life in Israel “simply impossible”, she explains, noting that most residents o...

stories.title: Literature in time of crisis: world-renowned writer Azar Nafisi speaks to France 24
stories.description: France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks with Azar Nafisi, Iranian-American writer, whose best-selling book "Reading Lolita in Tehran" remained on the New York Times bestseller list for over one hundred weeks. They discuss the role of literature and its subversive power in a time of war.

stories.title: US embassy attacked in Baghdad, after strikes kill Iran-backed fighters in Iraq
stories.description: The US embassy in Baghdad was hit by an attack on Saturday following strikes that killed three members of a powerful Iran-backed group in the capital, security sources said. A cloud of black smoke rose above the US diplomatic mission shortly after the sound of explosions on Satur...

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon are expected to hold direct talks in the coming days
stories.description: An overnight Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a dozen health workers at a clinic, with health authorities saying 26 paramedics have been killed since March 2. Israel and Lebanon ​are expected to hold direct talks in the coming days, their first since the start of ​the ‌I...

stories.title: Israel threatens to strike ambulances in Lebanon in Hezbollah fighting
stories.description: ⁠The Israeli military warned ​on Saturday that it may strike ambulances and medical facilities which it said were being used unlawfully by Hezbollah in Lebanon, ​though it did not provide evidence for the claim. At least 26 medics and first responders have been killed in Israeli ...

stories.title: Why did the US military bomb the strategic Iranian island of Kharg?
stories.description: President Donald Trump said US forces “obliterated” military targets on Iran’s Kharg Island. The piece of land is the beating heart of Iran’s oil export system, strategically located in the Persian Gulf. For decades, it has been essential in connecting Iran’s vast oil reserves wi...

stories.title: Why did the US bomb Kharg Island? What is its significance for Iran?
stories.description: The islands off Iran have become the latest focus of the war after a U.S strike destroyed military sites Friday on Kharg Island, which is vital to Iran's oil network. The US strike on the island in the Persian Gulf left its oil infrastructure intact, but President Donald Trump wa...

stories.title: Israeli strike kills 12 medics in a clinic in southern Lebanon
stories.description: An overnight Israeli strike killed a dozen medical staff at a clinic in south Lebanon, health authorities said earlier Saturday, after Iran-backed Hezbollah's leader Naim Qassem said his group was ready for a long confrontation with Israel. Lebanon was drawn into the Middle East ...

stories.title: Iraq: US embassy complex in Baghdad hit by a drone strike
stories.description: Explosions rocked cities across the Middle East on Saturday as the US embassy in Baghdad was hit by a drone strike and Tehran warned Emiratis to stay away from ports as it targets the world's energy supply lines. Details by Caroline Baum.

stories.title: Lebanon says 26 paramedics were killed in Israeli strikes since March 2
stories.description: Lebanon's health ministry said Saturday that Israeli strikes had killed 26 paramedics and wounded 51 others since conflict erupted between Israel and Hezbollah. The ministry statement also accused Israel of repeatedly "targeting ambulance crews while they were performing rescue d...

stories.title: Iran threatens United Arab Emirate's ports and docks
stories.description: Some oil-loading operations have been suspended in the United Arab Emirates' Fujairah emirate, ​a major ‌bunkering hub and crude export terminal, after a drone attack and fire ⁠on Saturday, industry and trade sources said. The suspension comes hours after the US attacked military...

stories.title: NATO defence systems shoot down Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey
stories.description: Turkey's defence ministry says a ballistic missile fired from Iran and heading into Turkish airspace after passing Syria and Iraq was destroyed by NATO air and missile defence systems in the eastern Mediterranean Sea. It's the third such incident since the war began. Turkey's pre...

stories.title: Two weeks into the war in the Middle East - What happens next?
stories.description: This Saturday marks two weeks since the US and Israel launched their first attacks on Iran. Where are we at in this conflict, what has it taught us, and where does it go from here? Scott Lucas, a Professor of US and International Politics at University College Dublin's Clinton In...

stories.title: French soldier killed by Iranian drone was on anti-jihad mission in Iraq
stories.description: The French officer killed by an Iranian-made drone on Thursday was part of a Western military contingent working to combat potential jihadists in Iraqi Kurdistan. The killing of Chief Warrant Officer Arnaud Frion marks France's first military death in the Middle East war.

stories.title: US military bombs military sites on Kharg island, Iranian media reports no damage
stories.description: President Donald Trump said the US destroyed military sites on an island vital to Iran’s oil network and warned that its oil infrastructure could be next if Iran continues to interfere with the passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Fars news agency, citing sources...

stories.title: US-Israeli strike kills 15 at Isfahan factory, Iranian media says
stories.description: Iranian media report the deaths in central Iran as Tehran launches new missile salvoes at Israeli targets.

stories.title: Thousands in Madrid protest ‘forgotten’ Gaza, warn Iran war may spiral into
stories.description: Thousands rallied in central Madrid calling for an end to the war involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

stories.title: Hundreds protest against US-Israeli strikes on Iran in Seoul
stories.description: Hundreds of peace activists and demonstrators marched in central Seoul on Saturday in protest of the US-Israeli strikes.

stories.title: Two more Iranian female footballers pull Australia asylum bids: State media
stories.description: Seven members of the Iranian football delegation sought asylum, with four now known to have withdrawn applications.

stories.title: Turkey on alert as Iran conflict escalates, warns against provocations
stories.description: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan says Ankara is monitoring the regional war involving Iran, the US, and Israel.

stories.title: UNESCO condemns strike on Minab girls’ school: ‘They attacked twice… How
stories.description: Secretary of Iran’s National Commission for UNESCO, condemned a missile strike on a girls’ primary school in Minab.

stories.title: Six killed in attacks on Ukraine as EU extends sanctions against Russians
stories.description: EU maintains pressure after slamming US for lifting sanctions on Russian oil exports as Middle East war bites.

stories.title: Trump administration threatens news outlets over critical coverage of Iran
stories.description: FCC chair says news outlets that share 'distortions' about US war with Iran could lose their licences.

stories.title: Iranian woman explains ‘Allahu Akbar’ to Americans, amid explosion in Iran
stories.description: Video shows an Iranian woman recording a message to Americans, amid an explosion in Iran.

stories.title: Iran warns UAE ports and ‘American hideouts’ are targets
stories.description: An Iranian military spokesperson warned that ports, docks and “American hideouts” in the UAE could be targets.

stories.title: Is the world ignoring Gaza?
stories.description: Amid the US and Israel's war on Iran, we discuss the impact on diplomatic efforts and support for Palestine.

stories.title: Trump says ‘many countries’ will send warships to Hormuz amid Iran blockade
stories.description: US president urges naval coalition as Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed to 'tankers and ships of enemies' during war.

stories.title: ‘Israel targeting Iran’s security forces may aim to encourage uprising’
stories.description: ‘Israel targeting Iran’s security forces may aim to encourage uprising’ says director of the Iran Program at the MEI.

stories.title: The Iranian moment: A leap into the unknown
stories.description: In the past century, Iran has undergone two major transformations. A third seems to be on its way.

stories.title: Iran allows limited Indian shipping through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Iran has allowed some Indian vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump claims ‘many countries’ will send warships to keep Strait of Hormuz open
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that many countries would send warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, but did not provide details on which countries would do so. “Many countries, especially those who are affected by Iran’s attempted closure of the Hormuz Strait, w...

stories.title: China urges Pakistan, Afghanistan to resolve tensions via talks, not force
stories.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi told his Afghan counterpart Amir Khan Muttaqi in a phone call that disputes between Afghanistan and Pakistan should be resolved through dialogue and consultation, not force, the foreign ministry said in a statement published on Friday. Wang urged ...

stories.title: Trump, Xi prepare to meet amid Iran war, uncertain goals
stories.description: Just over two weeks before President Donald Trump is due to meet his counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing, Chinese uncertainty over the US leader’s goals and the impact of the Iran war hangs over the highly anticipated state visit. Trump’s trip, his first to China in his second term...

stories.title: South Korea says North fired around 10 ballistic missiles
stories.description: North Korea fired about 10 unidentified ballistic missiles toward the Sea of Japan Saturday, Seoul’s Joint Chiefs of Staff (JCS) said, days after Pyongyang warned of “terrible consequences” over South Korea-US military drills. Pyongyang recently dashed hopes of a diplomatic thaw ...

stories.title: War displaces up to 3.2m Iranians internally: UNHCR
stories.description: ISLAMABAD: The United Nations refugee agency has said that between 600,000 and one million Iranian households have been temporarily displaced inside Iran as a result of US-Israeli war against the country. The UNHCR’s preliminary assessments represent displacement of up to 3.2 mil...

stories.title: Press review: Europe seeks to block US-Russia thaw as Russia eyes role in US-Iran talks
stories.description: Top stories from the Russian press on Friday, June 6th

stories.title: Israel to continue striking Beirut until Lebanon disarms Hezbollah — defense minister
stories.description: "Agreements must be honored and the Lebanese government does not do what is required, Israel will continue to act, and with great force," Israel Katz said

stories.title: Orban sees EU bracing for another decade of countering Russia amid proxy war in Ukraine
stories.description: Commenting on the Ukraine crisis, the Hungarian PM said that Brussels viewed Ukraine as "the first line of European defense" where pan-European interests were currently being defended

stories.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah targets in Beirut suburbs — IDF
stories.description: Prior to that, the Israeli army issued a warning for this neighborhood, telling residents to leave their homes immediately

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
